The Cultural Atlas is an educational resource providing comprehensive information on the cultural background of Australia’s migrant populations. The aim is to improve social cohesion and promote inclusion in an increasingly culturally diverse society.
There is no such thing as a ‘superior’ or ‘inferior’ culture, there are only various cultural patterns which make up a beautiful, multicoloured mosaic.

Our cross cultural training empowers your team to excel in cross-cultural collaboration, fostering effective communication and trust across diverse global teams. By mastering cultural nuances, employees build stronger relationships, reduce misunderstandings, and drive productivity in international settings.

Through expert-led courses, your workforce gains practical skills to navigate cultural differences, ensuring seamless global teamwork. This cultural competency enhances project outcomes, strengthens partnerships, and positions your organization for success in today’s interconnected world.

The name Euskal Herria is how the natives of the Basque Country refer to their land. It simply means, “land of the Basque speakers.” And Basque has been spoken by the feisty inhabitants well before the Romans ever arrived. Their full history as well as the origins of their language have long been shrouded in mystery.
However, their identity is very evident. This can be seen in the plethora of Basque music, dance, sports, festivals and food – all of which are celebrated with great fervor by the Basques.

Even with this strong identity, a new image of the Basque Country is emerging. Bilbao, which is now a mecca for design and architecture was, until some decades ago, a city full of decaying industrial complexes.

The name Euskal Herria is how the natives of the Basque Country refer to their land. It simply means, “land of the Basque speakers.” And Basque has been spoken by the feisty inhabitants well before the Romans ever arrived. Their full history as well as the origins of their language have long been shrouded in mystery.
However, their identity is very evident. This can be seen in the plethora of Basque music, dance, sports, festivals and food – all of which are celebrated with great fervor by the Basques.

Even with this strong identity, a new image of the Basque Country is emerging. Bilbao, which is now a mecca for design and architecture was, until some decades ago, a city full of decaying industrial complexes.

Zubi Eseki izenez ere ezaguna den Bizkaiko Zubia harribitxi arkitektonikoa da, Euskadiren industria eta itsas izaera ispilatzen dituena.
Portugalete eta Getxo batzen ditu zubiak, eta ondorioz, aukera paregabea da bi herri zoragarri horiek bisitatzeko. Atsegin handiz aterako dizkiozu argazkiak.
Arakatu, dastatu eta gozatu. Aurkitu ezazu Euskadi zeure kasa bizitzeko plan perfektua.
